inputs.name: iterator
inputs.description: The iterator object returned by the pub.parquet:getBatchIterator service.

inputs.name: docTypeName
inputs.description: The fully qualified name of an IS document type.

outputs.name: schema
outputs.description: A Parquet schema for the given IS document type.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Name of the Parquet file to be read.

outputs.name: iterator
outputs.description: An iterator object that can be used to iterate over the records in the Parquet file.

inputs.name: iterator
inputs.description: The iterator object from which the records have to be retrieved. This object is obtained from the pub.parquet:getBatchIterator service.

inputs.name: batchSize
inputs.description: Number of records to be fetched in the current iteration.

outputs.name: records
outputs.description: A batch of Parquet records converted to an IData array. The number of records returned is less than or equal to the batchSize parameter.

outputs.name: iterationStatus
outputs.description: Details about the iteration are provided in the following fields:

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Name of the Parquet file to be read.

outputs.name: records
outputs.description: An array of IData objects containing Parquet records. The tables Mapping of Parquet basic types to Integration Server data types and Mapping of Parquet logical types to Integration Server data types list how Parquet data types map to Integration Server data types.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Name of the Parquet file to which the records will be written.

inputs.name: schema
inputs.description: Optional. A Parquet schema against which the documents will be matched before the records are written to the file. If a schema is not provided, an IS document type must be provided using the docTypeName parameter. If any of provided documents do not conform to the given schema exactly, an exception is thrown.

inputs.name: docTypeName
inputs.description: Optional. The fully qualified name of an IS document type. A document type can be provided instead of the schema parameter. The document type is converted to a Parquet schema internally and used for converting and writing the Parquet file. The tables Mapping of Integration Serverdata types to Parquet basic types for write operations and Mapping of Integration Server data types to Parquet Logical types list how Integration Server data types map to Parquet schema types. Note: Either a schema or a docTypeName must be provided to validate the data before it is written to the Parquet file.

inputs.name: records
inputs.description: Array of IData objects to be written to the Parquet file.

inputs.name: options
inputs.description: Optional. Options such as compression methods can be passed to this service.
